hypothesis: If a small language model is continually pre-trained on MOF-focused literature and large chemical structure corpora and then fine-tuned on MOFid-to-property mappings, then it will achieve lower MAE than structure-based baselines on predicting MOF band gaps and gas adsorption—especially with limited (1–10k) fine-tuning data—because domain-specific textual pre-training captures transferable structure–property knowledge without requiring 3D inputs.
